english: Huaiyang Pork Meatball with Mushroom Core

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 250 g, comprising one large meatball with broth and cabbage)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart is one large braised pork meatball (approximately 250 g including broth and Napa cabbage), typically served as a main course at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork, ground, 72% lean / 28% fat, raw (FDC ID 168352) and Mushrooms, shiitake, cooked (FDC ID 169242); values weighted and adapted for this composite dish
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for fatty ground pork (FDC ID 168352), cooked shiitake mushrooms (FDC ID 169242), and Napa cabbage (FDC ID 169975), weighted to reflect the approximate composition of this dish (~75% fatty pork, ~15% mushroom, ~10% cabbage and binders). The input record's per-100g values (energy 185 kcal, protein 11.5 g, fat 14.2 g, carbohydrate 3.8 g) are consistent with this weighted estimate and are retained. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 5.2 g per 100 g based on USDA fatty pork profile. Sodium 380 mg per 100 g reflects seasoning with soy sauce and broth, consistent with USDA data for seasoned pork preparations.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate meat dish profile
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart. GI estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous profile of high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate braised meat dishes documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). The dish contains only 3.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, predominantly from mushrooms and a small starch binder; protein and fat dominate and substantially blunt glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (30 × 9.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 2.85, rounded to 2.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (380 mg per 100 g; ~950 mg per 250 g serving) from soy sauce seasoning and broth, combined with elevated saturated fat from fatty pork
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than one serving per occasion, request reduced-sodium broth and soy sauce, and avoid consuming the full volume of broth to minimize sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (3.8 g per 100 g) and low estimated GI (~30) result in minimal postprandial glucose excursion; high protein supports satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate load and low GI, but total energy and saturated fat intake should be monitored given the dish's energy density of 185 kcal per 100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~5.2 g per 100 g) from fatty pork and moderate sodium (380 mg per 100 g) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; mushrooms contribute beneficial beta-glucans and antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in moderate portions, preferring leaner pork preparations where available, and should account for the saturated fat and sodium contribution within their daily dietary targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High intake of fatty red meat and elevated sodium may be pro-inflammatory in susceptible individuals, though mushrooms provide antioxidant compounds
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consume this dish in moderation; the mushroom component provides beneficial antioxidants, but the overall saturated fat and sodium content may exacerbate inflammation in sensitive patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart (菌心狮子头, Jūn Xīn Shī Zi Tóu) is a gourmet variation of the classic Huaiyang cuisine dish 'Lion's Head' (狮子头, Shī Zi Tóu), originating from the Jiangsu province of East China. Traditional Lion's Head consists of very large, tender hand-chopped pork meatballs — typically made from a 70:30 lean-to-fat pork ratio — simmered slowly in a clear or lightly browned savory broth with Napa cabbage, the leafy cabbage representing the lion's mane. In this 'mushroom heart' (菌心) variation, each meatball is meticulously hand-formed around a concentrated core of diced and braised wild mushrooms, typically Porcini (Bolete) or Shiitake. This hidden mushroom center delivers a surprising burst of deep, earthy umami flavor and a firmer textural contrast against the melt-in-the-mouth surrounding pork. The dish is slow-cooked by simmering, allowing the mushroom essence to infuse both the meat and the surrounding broth. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 185 kcal, 11.5 g protein, 14.2 g fat (including ~5.2 g saturated fat), and only 3.8 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate dish. Sodium is relatively high at 380 mg per 100 g due to soy sauce and broth seasoning. The estimated glycemic index is low (~30), making it suitable for blood sugar management. The mushroom component contributes dietary fibre (1.2 g per 100 g), B vitamins, selenium, and antioxidant compounds. Clinically, the dish is appropriate for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low carbohydrate load, but individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should moderate intake due to sodium and saturated fat content. It is a celebratory centerpiece dish served at family gatherings and Huaiyang banquets.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for fatty ground pork, cooked shiitake mushrooms, and Napa cabbage, weighted to reflect the approximate composition of this dish. The input record's per-100g values are consistent with this weighted estimate. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database profile for high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate braised meat dishes. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at 5.2 g per 100 g based on USDA FoodData Central fatty ground pork profile (FDC ID 168352); not directly measured for this dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation, cholesterol, and moisture data for this specific composite Huaiyang dish preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2026-04-17. 3. GI value of 30 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Lion's Head with Mushroom Heart exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. The input record cited Yangzhou University Culinary Science Department as a source; this organization is not on the approved source list and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the primary nutrient evidence base. 5. Duplicate portion entry in the input record (two identical '1 serving (250g)' entries) has been deduplicated to a single 250 g serving. 6. Halal status is marked false as traditional preparation uses pork; consumers following halal dietary requirements should avoid this dish. 7. The dish is not suitable for vegetarian or vegan diets due to pork content.
